task_completion.description: Open TopoCal and attempt to load the survey dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The TopoCal application is open, and there is evidence of the agent opening or attempting to import 'C:\workspace\data\intersection_asbuilt.xyz'.

task_completion.description: Draw polylines connecting the corners for Ramp A, Ramp B, and Ramp C.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The CAD drawing area in TopoCal shows polylines forming three distinct quadrilateral shapes representing the three ramps.

task_completion.description: Annotate the CAD drawing with 'PASS' or 'FAIL' text for each ramp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Text labels reading 'PASS' or 'FAIL' are visible on or adjacent to the drawn ramps in the TopoCal drawing area.

task_completion.description: Export the final drawing to the specified DXF file path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save or export dialog in TopoCal showing the file being saved exactly as 'C:\workspace\data\ada_drawing.dxf', or a file explorer showing this file was created.

task_completion.description: Save a text report listing the status of each ramp.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A text editor or save dialog showing a report containing the pass/fail status of Ramps A, B, and C being saved to 'C:\workspace\data\ada_report.txt'.

integrity.description: The agent used the TopoCal graphical user interface to perform the CAD tasks.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with TopoCal's menus, toolbars, and drawing area to create polylines and text, rather than using a terminal script to generate the DXF.

integrity.description: The agent manually created or exported the text report based on its findings.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent is seen typing or formatting the text report in an editor, rather than copying a pre-existing file via terminal commands.

integrity.description: The agent did not use terminal commands to bypass the application UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent does not use 'echo' or python scripts in the terminal to blindly generate the ada_drawing.dxf and ada_report.txt files without interacting with TopoCal.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The expected text report path is 'C:\workspace\data\ada_report.txt' and the expected DXF drawing path is 'C:\workspace\data\ada_drawing.dxf'. Note: The underlying survey data file ('intersection_asbuilt.xyz') is known to be missing from the environment. The agent may encounter errors loading the data. Score the agent based on its genuine attempts to complete the drawing, annotation, and exporting steps using the TopoCal GUI and text editors, even if it has to make assumptions due to the missing data.
